title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Go Client Systems · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http representation negotiation and validation boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Field Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:2. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Field Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Field Semantics, keep Field Semantics connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

At Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Language Charset Negotiation, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before running it: Go staging control-plane client asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:5. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Language Charset Negotiation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: JSON, text, bytes, Unicode code points, and user-perceived characters are interchangeable. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Language Charset Negotiation happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Language Charset Negotiation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Language Charset Negotiation

Competency probe: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go Defend the transferred model · Workshop Language Charset Negotiation, keep Language Charset Negotiation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret charset and language metadata, negotiation quality values, and Unicode boundaries without locale guesses. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
